name: Kupfergrube
description: Famous for copper-rich volcanic soil producing spicy, mineral Rieslings with exotic fruit. Name means 'copper mine.'
historicalNotes: Copper mining occurred here for centuries. Dönnhoff makes legendary examples from parcels throughout the vineyard.

name: Felsenberg
description: Steep volcanic slopes producing taut, mineral-driven Rieslings with great aging potential. Benchmark Nahe terroir.
historicalNotes: Helmut Dönnhoff pioneered dry Riesling from this site in the 1980s. Name means 'rock mountain.'

name: Hermannshöhle
description: Most famous vineyard in Nahe. Produces elegant, precise Rieslings with remarkable finesse and longevity.
historicalNotes: Named after Hermann cave. Considered equal to top Mosel sites. Dönnhoff and Schäfer-Fröhlich excel here.

name: Felseneck
description: Small, steep volcanic site producing powerful, mineral Rieslings. One of Nahe's steepest vineyards.
historicalNotes: Schäfer-Fröhlich owns significant parcels. Extremely challenging to farm due to steepness.

name: Dellchen
description: Small, prestigious site producing elegant Rieslings with great depth and minerality.
historicalNotes: Dönnhoff makes benchmark examples. Historically part of royal Prussian domain.

name: Bastei
description: Steep, rocky vineyard producing powerful, spicy Rieslings with exotic character.
historicalNotes: Name refers to rock bastion. Extreme slope makes it one of Nahe's most challenging sites to work.

name: Pittermannchen
description: Volcanic terroir producing aromatic, spicy Rieslings with great depth. One of Dorsheim's finest sites.
historicalNotes: Schäfer-Fröhlich and Gut Hermannsberg make outstanding examples.

name: Steinberg
description: Classic Nahe site producing mineral-driven Rieslings with excellent structure and aging potential.
historicalNotes: Historically part of the Royal Prussian wine domain established in 1902.
